<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Meme generator feature",
  "overview": "Scaffold a small web app in this empty repo and implement a client-side meme generator: pick or upload an image, add classic top/bottom caption text, preview on canvas, and download a PNG—no backend required.",
  "plan": "# Meme generator (greenfield web MVP)\n\n## Context\n\n[CursorAI-training](/home/tusharsaini/CursorAI-training) currently has **no application code**—only empty [&#96;Phase-1/&#96;](Phase-1/) and [&#96;Phase-2/&#96;](Phase-2/) folders. This plan **introduces a minimal web app** and the meme feature in one slice. If you instead want **Android (Kotlin)**, say so before implementation; the flow is the same (bitmap + draw text + save), but files and APIs differ entirely.\n\n## Recommended stack (minimal dependencies)\n\n- **Vite + TypeScript** at repo root (or under &#96;Phase-1/meme-app/&#96; if you want phases kept separate—your choice at implementation time).\n- **HTML5 Canvas 2D** for compositing: draw image scaled to fit, then draw two text lines with stroke + fill (“Impact”-style meme look).\n- **Browser APIs only** for file input (&#96;\u003cinput type=\"file\"\u003e&#96;) and download (&#96;canvas.toBlob&#96; + object URL). No new runtime libraries required beyond Vite/TS tooling.\n\n## User flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  upload[Upload_or_template]\n  edit[Edit_top_bottom_text]\n  canvas[Redraw_canvas]\n  dl[Download_PNG]\n  upload --\u003e edit --\u003e canvas --\u003e dl\n  edit --\u003e canvas\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Implementation outline\n\n1. **Scaffold**  \n   - Add &#96;package.json&#96;, &#96;vite.config.ts&#96;, &#96;tsconfig.json&#96;, &#96;index.html&#96;, and a single entry (e.g. [&#96;src/main.ts&#96;](src/main.ts)) that mounts UI.\n\n2. **UI** (keep it small: one screen)  \n   - File picker for image (accept &#96;image/*&#96;).  \n   - Optional: 2–3 bundled placeholder images in [&#96;public/templates/&#96;](public/templates/) as quick-start buttons.  \n   - Two text inputs: top caption, bottom caption.  \n   - Buttons: **Download** (and optionally **Reset**).\n\n3. **Canvas logic** (core feature)  \n   - Load &#96;Image&#96; from file or URL; on load, compute draw dimensions (letterbox or cover—pick one and document).  \n   - Draw image, then draw text with:\n     - Large font, centered per line, max width with word wrap or scale-to-fit (simple approach: split words and reduce font size until it fits).  \n     - White fill + black stroke for readability.  \n   - On any input change, debounce redraw (e.g. &#96;requestAnimationFrame&#96; or 50–100 ms debounce).\n\n4. **Download**  \n   - &#96;canvas.toBlob('image/png')&#96; → temporary object URL → &#96;\u003ca download\u003e&#96; click, revoke URL.\n\n5. **Quality bar**  \n   - Basic responsive layout (canvas max-width 100%).  \n   - Clear empty states (“Choose an image to start”).  \n   - No secrets, no &#96;eval&#96;; images stay in the browser unless you add a backend later.\n\n## Files to add (indicative)\n\n| Area | Files |\n|------|--------|\n| Tooling | &#96;package.json&#96;, &#96;vite.config.ts&#96;, &#96;tsconfig.json&#96;, &#96;index.html&#96; |\n| App | &#96;src/main.ts&#96;, &#96;src/style.css&#96; (or minimal inline if you prefer) |\n| Logic | &#96;src/meme_canvas.ts&#96; — load image, layout, draw, export |\n| Assets | &#96;public/templates/*.jpg&#96; (optional) |\n\n## Tests (lightweight)\n\n- **Vitest** + **happy-dom** or **jsdom** to unit-test pure functions: e.g. line wrapping / font-size reduction given a fixed “canvas width”—without needing a real browser image if you inject a tiny mock canvas or test only the text-layout math in isolation.  \n- If you want zero test deps initially, add a short manual test checklist in the PR description instead (acceptable for a training repo if you skip Vitest).\n\n## Out of scope for MVP (can extend later)\n\n- User accounts, cloud storage, or AI-generated captions.  \n- Drag-to-position text boxes (would need hit-testing or a small canvas library).  \n- Android app (separate module if you choose Kotlin later).\n\n## Branching (if using Cloud Agent rules)\n\nWork on a branch such as &#96;cursor/meme-generator-web&#96;, not directly on &#96;main&#96;.\n",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Add Vite + TypeScript scaffold (package.json, vite.config, index.html, src/main.ts)",
      "id": "scaffold-vite",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Implement meme_canvas.ts: image load, draw, Impact-style text, wrap/scale, redraw hook",
      "id": "meme-canvas",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Wire file input, top/bottom fields, canvas element, download button + optional templates",
      "id": "ui-wire",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add minimal CSS, optional Vitest for layout helpers or document manual test steps",
      "id": "polish-test",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```
